heading: 9.7.16.10.8.4.Sparsity selector
content:
The value of the sparsity selector selects the sub-columns in the Tensor Memory
to form the sparsity metadata matrix, which is used with matrix `A` to form the
multiplicand matrix.

The following shows the sparse metadata matrix layout in Tensor Memory for various MMA variants:

###### 9.7.16.10.8.4.1. [Layout of the Sparsity Metadata Matrix for M = 64 for `.kind::f16`](#tcgen05-sparse-matrices-sparsity-selector-kind-f16-m64)

[Figure 262](#tcgen05-sparse-matrices-sparsity-selector-kind-f16-m64-dig) shows which sub-columns gets
selected for different values of Sparsity Selector.



[Figure: tcgen05-sparse-matrices-sparsity-selector-kind-f16-m64]
SPARSITY METADATA LAYOUT FOR M=64, KIND::F16

OVERVIEW:
This figure describes the sparsity metadata layout for structured sparse MMA operations with M=64 and f16 data type. The layout shows how 32 threads (lanes 0-31, shown as rows) each hold a 32-bit metadata register, and how the bits within that register map to specific matrix rows and column ranges.

FIGURE STRUCTURE:
- Rows 0-15 on the left axis represent thread lanes (lane 0 through lane 15)
- Rows 16-31 are shown but empty (lanes 16-31 hold no metadata for this half of the warp, or the pattern repeats)
- There are 16 columns total in the grid, split into two halves of 8 columns each
- Left half (columns 1-8): one group of 8 metadata entries per lane
- Right half (columns 9-16): second group of 8 metadata entries per lane
- The notation in each cell is: "row:start_col-end_col"

COLOR CODING:
- Green cells: Sparsity Selector = 00
- Blue/teal cells: Sparsity Selector = 01

BIT WIDTH ANNOTATIONS (shown below the grid, indicating cumulative bit widths spanning columns):
- 4-bits: spans columns 1-2 (first two columns)
- 8-bits: spans columns 1-4 (first four columns)
- 16-bits: spans columns 1-8 (first eight columns)
- 32-bits: spans columns 1-16 (all sixteen columns)

CELL NOTATION FORMAT:
Each cell contains "R:C1-C2" where R = matrix row index, C1-C2 = column range (start_col through end_col).

COMPLETE CELL DATA (Lane : Column_position -> Cell_content):

LEFT HALF OF GRID (columns 1-8, green/light colored):

Lane 0:  col1=0:0-1,   col2=0:2-3,   col3=0:4-5,   col4=0:6-7,   col5=8:0-1,   col6=8:2-3,   col7=8:4-5,   col8=8:6-7
Lane 1:  col1=1:0-1,   col2=1:2-3,   col3=1:4-5,   col4=1:6-7,   col5=9:0-1,   col6=9:2-3,   col7=9:4-5,   col8=9:6-7
Lane 2:  col1=2:0-1,   col2=2:2-3,   col3=2:4-5,   col4=2:6-7,   col5=10:0-1,  col6=10:2-3,  col7=10:4-5,  col8=10:6-7
Lane 3:  col1=3:0-1,   col2=3:2-3,   col3=3:4-5,   col4=3:6-7,   col5=11:0-1,  col6=11:2-3,  col7=11:4-5,  col8=11:6-7
Lane 4:  col1=4:0-1,   col2=4:2-3,   col3=4:4-5,   col4=4:6-7,   col5=12:0-1,  col6=12:2-3,  col7=12:4-5,  col8=12:6-7
Lane 5:  col1=5:0-1,   col2=5:2-3,   col3=5:4-5,   col4=5:6-7,   col5=13:0-1,  col6=13:2-3,  col7=13:4-5,  col8=13:6-7
Lane 6:  col1=6:0-1,   col2=6:2-3,   col3=6:4-5,   col4=6:6-7,   col5=14:0-1,  col6=14:2-3,  col7=14:4-5,  col8=14:6-7
Lane 7:  col1=7:0-1,   col2=7:2-3,   col3=7:4-5,   col4=7:6-7,   col5=15:0-1,  col6=15:2-3,  col7=15:4-5,  col8=15:6-7
Lane 8:  col1=0:8-9,   col2=0:10-11, col3=0:12-13, col4=0:14-15, col5=8:8-9,   col6=8:10-11, col7=8:12-13, col8=8:14-15
Lane 9:  col1=1:8-9,   col2=1:10-11, col3=1:12-13, col4=1:14-15, col5=9:8-9,   col6=9:10-11, col7=9:12-13, col8=9:14-15
Lane 10: col1=2:8-9,   col2=2:10-11, col3=2:12-13, col4=2:14-15, col5=10:8-9,  col6=10:10-11,col7=10:12-13,col8=10:14-15
Lane 11: col1=3:8-9,   col2=3:10-11, col3=3:12-13, col4=3:14-15, col5=11:8-9,  col6=11:10-11,col7=11:12-13,col8=11:14-15
Lane 12: col1=4:8-9,   col2=4:10-11, col3=4:12-13, col4=4:14-15, col5=12:8-9,  col6=12:10-11,col7=12:12-13,col8=12:14-15
Lane 13: col1=5:8-9,   col2=5:10-11, col3=5:12-13, col4=5:14-15, col5=13:8-9,  col6=13:10-11,col7=13:12-13,col8=13:14-15
Lane 14: col1=6:8-9,   col2=6:10-11, col3=6:12-13, col4=6:14-15, col5=14:8-9,  col6=14:10-11,col7=14:12-13,col8=14:14-15
Lane 15: col1=7:8-9,   col2=7:10-11, col3=7:12-13, col4=7:14-15, col5=15:8-9,  col6=15:10-11,col7=15:12-13,col8=15:14-15

RIGHT HALF OF GRID (columns 9-16, blue/teal colored):

Lane 0:  col9=0:0-1,   col10=0:2-3,  col11=0:4-5,  col12=0:6-7,  col13=8:0-1,  col14=8:2-3,  col15=8:4-5,  col16=8:6-7
Lane 1:  col9=1:0-1,   col10=1:2-3,  col11=1:4-5,  col12=1:6-7,  col13=9:0-1,  col14=9:2-3,  col15=9:4-5,  col16=9:6-7
Lane 2:  col9=2:0-1,   col10=2:2-3,  col11=2:4-5,  col12=2:6-7,  col13=10:0-1, col14=10:2-3, col15=10:4-5, col16=10:6-7
Lane 3:  col9=3:0-1,   col10=3:2-3,  col11=3:4-5,  col12=3:6-7,  col13=11:0-1, col14=11:2-3, col15=11:4-5, col16=11:6-7
Lane 4:  col9=4:0-1,   col10=4:2-3,  col11=4:4-5,  col12=4:6-7,  col13=12:0-1, col14=12:2-3, col15=12:4-5, col16=12:6-7
Lane 5:  col9=5:0-1,   col10=5:2-3,  col11=5:4-5,  col12=5:6-7,  col13=13:0-1, col14=13:2-3, col15=13:4-5, col16=13:6-7
Lane 6:  col9=6:0-1,   col10=6:2-3,  col11=6:4-5,  col12=6:6-7,  col13=14:0-1, col14=14:2-3, col15=14:4-5, col16=14:6-7
Lane 7:  col9=7:0-1,   col10=7:2-3,  col11=7:4-5,  col12=7:6-7,  col13=15:0-1, col14=15:2-3, col15=15:4-5, col16=15:6-7
Lane 8:  col9=0:8-9,   col10=0:10-11,col11=0:12-13,col12=0:14-15,col13=8:8-9,  col14=8:10-11,col15=


Figure 262 Sparsity Metadata Layout for M = 64 for `.kind::f16`

###### 9.7.16.10.8.4.2. [Layout of the Sparsity Metadata Matrix for M = 128 / M = 256 for `.kind::f16`](#tcgen05-sparse-matrices-sparsity-selector-kind-f16-m128-256)

[Figure 263](#tcgen05-sparse-matrices-sparsity-selector-kind-f16-m128-256-dig) shows which sub-columns gets
selected for different values of Sparsity Selector.



[Figure: tcgen05-sparse-matrices-sparsity-selector-kind-f16-m128-256]
SPARSITY METADATA LAYOUT FOR M=128 / M=256 FOR .kind::f16

OVERVIEW:
This figure shows the sparsity metadata layout for sparse MMA operations with .kind::f16 (16-bit floating point). The layout covers 32 threads (lanes 0-31) across 16 columns of metadata cells. The figure is divided into two halves by sparsity selector value: the left 8 columns correspond to Sparsity Selector = 00 (green background) and the right 8 columns correspond to Sparsity Selector = 01 (cyan/blue background).

CELL NOTATION FORMAT:
Each cell is labeled as "lane:start_col-end_col" where lane is the thread lane number (0-31), start_col and end_col are the bit positions within the metadata register held by that thread.

BIT WIDTH GROUPINGS (shown by arrows at bottom):
- 4-bits: spans first column group
- 8-bits: spans first two column groups
- 16-bits: spans first four column groups (columns 1-4 of Sparsity Selector 00 half)
- 32-bits: spans all eight columns of the Sparsity Selector 00 half (full 32-bit word)

COLUMN STRUCTURE:
There are 16 total columns. Each half (Sparsity Selector 00 and Sparsity Selector 01) has 8 columns with the following bit ranges per column:
Column 1: bits 0-3
Column 2: bits 4-7
Column 3: bits 8-11
Column 4: bits 12-15
Column 5: bits 0-3 (of second 16-bit group, i.e., bits 16-19 in 32-bit context shown as 8:0-3 etc.)
Column 6: bits 4-7
Column 7: bits 8-11
Column 8: bits 12-15

DETAILED THREAD-TO-METADATA MAPPING:

The cells encode as "lane_number:bit_start-bit_end". The lane number prefix before the colon indicates which thread (lane 0-31) holds that metadata. The bit range after the colon indicates which bits within that thread's metadata register.

SPARSITY SELECTOR = 00 (left 8 columns, green):

Rows 0-7 (threads 0-7), first 4 columns (bits 0-15):
Row 0 (thread 0):  0:0-3  | 0:4-7  | 0:8-11  | 0:12-15
Row 1 (thread 1):  1:0-3  | 1:4-7  | 1:8-11  | 1:12-15
Row 2 (thread 2):  2:0-3  | 2:4-7  | 2:8-11  | 2:12-15
Row 3 (thread 3):  3:0-3  | 3:4-7  | 3:8-11  | 3:12-15
Row 4 (thread 4):  4:0-3  | 4:4-7  | 4:8-11  | 4:12-15
Row 5 (thread 5):  5:0-3  | 5:4-7  | 5:8-11  | 5:12-15
Row 6 (thread 6):  6:0-3  | 6:4-7  | 6:8-11  | 6:12-15
Row 7 (thread 7):  7:0-3  | 7:4-7  | 7:8-11  | 7:12-15

Rows 0-7 (threads 0-7), columns 5-8 (second 16-bit group):
Row 0 (thread 0):  8:0-3  | 8:4-7  | 8:8-11  | 8:12-15
Row 1 (thread 1):  9:0-3  | 9:4-7  | 9:8-11  | 9:12-15
Row 2 (thread 2): 10:0-3  |10:4-7  |10:8-11  |10:12-15
Row 3 (thread 3): 11:0-3  |11:4-7  |11:8-11  |11:12-15
Row 4 (thread 4): 12:0-3  |12:4-7  |12:8-11  |12:12-15
Row 5 (thread 5): 13:0-3  |13:4-7  |13:8-11  |13:12-15
Row 6 (thread 6): 14:0-3  |14:4-7  |14:8-11  |14:12-15
Row 7 (thread 7): 15:0-3  |15:4-7  |15:8-11  |15:12-15

Rows 8-15 (threads 0-7 second group), first 4 columns:
Row 8  (thread 0):  0:16-19 | 0:20-23 | 0:24-27 | 0:28-31
Row 9  (thread 1):  1:16-19 | 1:20-23 | 1:24-27 | 1:28-31
Row 10 (thread 2):  2:16-19 | 2:20-23 | 2:24-27 | 2:28-31
Row 11 (thread 3):  3:16-19 | 3:20-23 | 3:24-27 | 3:28-31
Row 12 (thread 4):  4:16-19 | 4:20-23 | 4:24-27 | 4:28-31
Row 13 (thread 5):  5:16-19 | 5:20-23 | 5:24-27 | 5:28-31
Row 14 (thread 6):  6:16-19 | 6:20-23 | 6:24-27 | 6:28-31
Row 15 (thread 7):  7:16-19 | 7:20-23 | 7:24-27 | 7:28-31

Rows 8-15, columns 5-8:
Row 8  (thread 0):  8:16-19 | 8:20-23 | 8:24-27 | 8:28-31
Row 9  (thread 1):  9:16-19 | 9:20-23 | 9:24-27 | 9:28-31
Row 10 (thread 2): 10:16-19 |10:20-23 |10:24-27 |10:28-31
Row 11 (thread 3): 11:16-19 |11:20-23 |11:24-27 |11:28-31
Row 12 (thread 4): 12:16-19 |12:20-23 |12:24-27 |12:28-31
Row 13 (thread 5): 13:16-19 |13:20-23 |13:24-27 |13:28-31
Row 14 (thread 6): 14:16-19 |14:20-23 |14:24-27 |14:28-31
Row 15 (thread 7): 15:16-19 |15:20-23 |15:24-27 |15:28-31

Rows 16-23 (threads 16-23 range), first 4 columns:
Row 16 (thread 0): 16:0-3  |16:4-7  |16:8-11  |16:12-15
Row 17 (thread 1): 17:0-3  |17:4-7  |17:8-11  |17:12-15
Row 18 (thread 2): 18:0-3  |18:4-7  |18:8-11  |18:12-15
Row 19 (thread 3): 19:0-3  |19:4-7  |19:8-11  |19:12-15
Row 20 (thread 4): 20:0-3  |20:4-7  |20:8-11  |20:12-15
Row 21 (thread 5): 21:0-3  |21:4-7  |21:8-11  |21:12-15
Row 22 (thread 6): 22:0-3  |22:4-7  |22:8-11  |22:12-15
Row 23 (thread 7): 23:0-3  |23:4-7  |23:8-11  |23:12-15

Rows 16-23, columns 5-8:
Row 16: 24:0-3  |24:4-7  |24:8-11  |24:12-15
Row 17: 25:0-3  |25:4-7  |25:8-11  |25:12-15
Row 18: 26:0-3  |26:4-7  |26:8-11  |26:12-15
Row 19: 27:0-3  |27:4-7  |27:8-11  |27:12-15
Row 20: 28:0-3  |28:4-7  |28:8-11  |28:12-15
Row 21: 29:0-3  |29:4-7  |29:8-11  |29:12-15
Row 22: 30:0-3  |30:4-7  |30:8-11  |30:12-15
Row 23: 31:0-3  |31:4-7  |31:8-11  |31:12-15

Rows 24-31, first 4 columns:
Row 24:


Figure 263 Sparsity Metadata Layout for M = 128 / M = 256 for `.kind::f16`

###### 9.7.16.10.8.4.3. [Layout of the Sparsity Metadata Matrix for M = 64 for `.kind::tf32`](#tcgen05-sparse-matrices-sparsity-selector-kind-tf32-m64)

[Figure 264](#tcgen05-sparse-matrices-sparsity-selector-kind-tf32-m64-dig) shows which sub-columns gets
selected for different values of Sparsity Selector.



[Figure: tcgen05-sparse-matrices-sparsity-selector-kind-tf32-m64]
SPARSITY METADATA LAYOUT FOR M=64, kind::tf32

OVERVIEW:
This figure describes the sparsity metadata layout for structured sparse MMA operations with M=64 and tf32 data type. The layout shows 16 rows (rows 0-15 are populated; rows 16-31 are empty) and 16 columns of metadata entries. Only rows 0-15 contain data.

NOTATION FORMAT:
Each cell is labeled as: lane : start_col - end_col
Where "lane" is the thread lane number (0-15), and "start_col - end_col" indicates the bit range within that lane's metadata register.

COLOR CODING:
- Green cells: Sparsity Selector = 00
- Cyan/Teal cells: Sparsity Selector = 01
- White/uncolored cells: also present (alternating pattern)

BIT WIDTH INDICATORS (shown at bottom):
- 4-bits: smallest unit per entry
- 8-bits: two entries combined
- 16-bits: four entries combined (spans first 4 columns of a group)
- 32-bits: eight entries combined (spans all 8 columns of a group)

GRID STRUCTURE:
The grid has 16 data rows (0-15) and 16 columns. The 16 columns are divided into two major groups of 8 columns each:
- Left group of 8 columns (columns 1-8 in the figure)
- Right group of 8 columns (columns 9-16 in the figure)

Each group of 8 columns is further subdivided into two sub-groups of 4 columns:
- Sub-group A (green, Sparsity Selector = 00): first 4 columns
- Sub-group B (cyan, Sparsity Selector = 01): next 4 columns

COMPLETE CELL DATA (Row : Col1 | Col2 | Col3 | Col4 | Col5 | Col6 | Col7 | Col8 | Col9 | Col10 | Col11 | Col12 | Col13 | Col14 | Col15 | Col16):

Row 0:  0:0-3   | 0:4-7   | 0:8-11  | 0:12-15 | 8:0-3   | 8:4-7   | 8:8-11  | 8:12-15 | 0:0-3   | 0:4-7   | 0:8-11  | 0:12-15 | 8:0-3   | 8:4-7   | 8:8-11  | 8:12-15
Row 1:  1:0-3   | 1:4-7   | 1:8-11  | 1:12-15 | 9:0-3   | 9:4-7   | 9:8-11  | 9:12-15 | 1:0-3   | 1:4-7   | 1:8-11  | 1:12-15 | 9:0-3   | 9:4-7   | 9:8-11  | 9:12-15
Row 2:  2:0-3   | 2:4-7   | 2:8-11  | 2:12-15 | 10:0-3  | 10:4-7  | 10:8-11 | 10:12-15| 2:0-3   | 2:4-7   | 2:8-11  | 2:12-15 | 10:0-3  | 10:4-7  | 10:8-11 | 10:12-15
Row 3:  3:0-3   | 3:4-7   | 3:8-11  | 3:12-15 | 11:0-3  | 11:4-7  | 11:8-11 | 11:12-15| 3:0-3   | 3:4-7   | 3:8-11  | 3:12-15 | 11:0-3  | 11:4-7  | 11:8-11 | 11:12-15
Row 4:  4:0-3   | 4:4-7   | 4:8-11  | 4:12-15 | 12:0-3  | 12:4-7  | 12:8-11 | 12:12-15| 4:0-3   | 4:4-7   | 4:8-11  | 4:12-15 | 12:0-3  | 12:4-7  | 12:8-11 | 12:12-15
Row 5:  5:0-3   | 5:4-7   | 5:8-11  | 5:12-15 | 13:0-3  | 13:4-7  | 13:8-11 | 13:12-15| 5:0-3   | 5:4-7   | 5:8-11  | 5:12-15 | 13:0-3  | 13:4-7  | 13:8-11 | 13:12-15
Row 6:  6:0-3   | 6:4-7   | 6:8-11  | 6:12-15 | 14:0-3  | 14:4-7  | 14:8-11 | 14:12-15| 6:0-3   | 6:4-7   | 6:8-11  | 6:12-15 | 14:0-3  | 14:4-7  | 14:8-11 | 14:12-15
Row 7:  7:0-3   | 7:4-7   | 7:8-11  | 7:12-15 | 15:0-3  | 15:4-7  | 15:8-11 | 15:12-15| 7:0-3   | 7:4-7   | 7:8-11  | 7:12-15 | 15:0-3  | 15:4-7  | 15:8-11 | 15:12-15
Row 8:  0:16-19 | 0:20-23 | 0:24-27 | 0:28-31 | 8:16-19 | 8:20-23 | 8:24-27 | 8:28-31 | 0:16-19 | 0:20-23 | 0:24-27 | 0:28-31 | 8:16-19 | 8:20-23 | 8:24-27 | 8:28-31
Row 9:  1:16-19 | 1:20-23 | 1:24-27 | 1:28-31 | 9:16-19 | 9:20-23 | 9:24-27 | 9:28-31 | 1:16-19 | 1:20-23 | 1:24-27 | 1:28-31 | 9:16-19 | 9:20-23 | 9:24-27 | 9:28-31
Row 10: 2:16-19 | 2:20-23 | 2:24-27 | 2:28-31 | 10:16-19| 10:20-23| 10:24-27| 10:28-31| 2:16-19 | 2:20-23 | 2:24-27 | 2:28-31 | 10:16-19| 10:20-23| 10:24-27| 10:28-31
Row 11: 3:16-19 | 3:20-23 | 3:24-27 | 3:28-31 | 11:16-19| 11:20-23| 11:24-27| 11:28-31| 3:16-19 | 3:20-23 | 3:24-27 | 3:28-31 | 11:16-19| 11:20-23| 11:24-27| 11:28-31
Row 12: 4:16-19 | 4:20-23 | 4:24-27 | 4:28-31 | 12:16-19| 12:20-23| 12:24-27| 12:28-31| 4:16-19 | 4:20-23 | 4:24-27 | 4:28-31 | 12:16-19| 12:20-23| 12:24-27| 12:28-31
Row 13: 5:16-19 | 5:20-23 | 5:24-27 | 5:28-31 | 13:16-19| 13:20-23| 13:24-27| 13:28-31| 5:16-19 | 5:20-23 | 5:24-27 | 5:28-31 | 13:16-19| 13:20-23| 13:24-27| 13:28-31
Row 14: 6:16-19 | 6:20-23 | 6:24-27 | 6:28-31 | 14:16-19| 14:20-23| 14:24-27| 14:28-31| 6:16-19 | 6:20-23 | 6:24-27 | 6:28-31 | 14:16-19| 14:20-23| 14:24-27| 14:28-31
Row 15: 7:16-19 | 7:20-23 | 7:24-27 | 7:28-31 | 15:16-19| 15:20-23| 15:24-27| 15:28-31| 7:16-


Figure 264 Sparsity Metadata Layout for M = 64 for `.kind::tf32`

###### 9.7.16.10.8.4.4. [Layout of the Sparsity Metadata Matrix for M = 128 / M = 256 for `.kind::tf32`](#tcgen05-sparse-matrices-sparsity-selector-kind-tf32-m128-256)

[Figure 265](#tcgen05-sparse-matrices-sparsity-selector-kind-tf32-m128-256-dig) shows which sub-columns gets
selected for different values of Sparsity Selector.



[Figure: tcgen05-sparse-matrices-sparsity-selector-kind-tf32-m128-256]
SPARSITY METADATA LAYOUT FOR M=128 / M=256 FOR .kind::tf32

OVERVIEW:
This figure shows the sparsity metadata layout for structured sparse MMA operations using tf32 data type. The layout covers 32 threads (lanes 0-31) across 16 columns of metadata entries. The figure is divided into two halves horizontally: the left 8 columns use Sparsity Selector = 00 (green background) and the right 8 columns use Sparsity Selector = 01 (cyan/teal background).

MMA SHAPE AND DATA TYPE:
- Data type: tf32 (.kind::tf32)
- M dimensions: M=128 or M=256
- Structured sparsity: 2:4 sparse format (2 non-zero elements out of every 4)

GRID STRUCTURE:
- Rows: 32 rows corresponding to thread lanes 0 through 31
- Columns: 16 total columns (0 through 15)
- Left half (columns 0-7): Sparsity Selector = 00 (green)
- Right half (columns 8-15): Sparsity Selector = 01 (cyan)

BIT WIDTH ANNOTATIONS (shown at bottom left):
- 4-bits: covers the first column (column 0)
- 8-bits: covers columns 0-1
- 16-bits: covers columns 0-3
- 32-bits: covers columns 0-7 (the full Sparsity Selector = 00 half)

CELL NOTATION FORMAT:
Each cell contains a value in the format "lane:start_col-end_col" where:
- lane = the row/thread lane number (0-31)
- start_col = starting column index of the metadata segment
- end_col = ending column index of the metadata segment

COMPLETE THREAD-TO-METADATA MAPPING:

The notation in each cell follows the pattern: ROW_NUMBER : COL_RANGE

Column groupings per row (reading left to right across all 16 columns):

SPARSITY SELECTOR = 00 (columns 0-7, green):

Lane 0:  0:0-1  | 0:2-3  | 0:4-5  | 0:6-7  | 8:0-1  | 8:2-3  | 8:4-5  | 8:6-7

###### 9.7.16.10.8.4.5. [Layout of the Sparsity Metadata Matrix for M = 64 for `.kind::f8f6f4`, `.kind::mxf8f6f4`, `.kind::i8`, `.kind::mxf4`, `.kind::mxf4nvf4`](#tcgen05-sparse-matrices-sparsity-selector-kind-f8f6f4-mxf8f6f4-m64)

The value of the sparsity selector:

* must be 0 for `.kind::i8` and `.kind::f8f6f4`
* is assumed to be 0 for `.kind::mxf8f6f4`, `.kind::mxf4` and `.kind::mxf4nvf4`

and all of the columns are selected as
shown in [Figure 266](#tcgen05-sparse-matrices-sparsity-selector-kind-f8f6f4-mxf8f6f4-m64-dig)



[Figure: tcgen05-sparse-matrices-sparsity-selector-kind-f8f6f4-mxf8f6f4-m64]
SPARSITY METADATA LAYOUT FOR M=64, APPLICABLE TO .kind::f8f6f4, .kind::mxf8f6f4, .kind::i8, .kind::mxf4, .kind::mxf4nvf4

==============================================================
GENERAL STRUCTURE
==============================================================

The figure shows a 2D grid where:
- Rows (left axis, labeled 0-31): Thread lane IDs within a warp (only lanes 0-15 are populated with data; lanes 16-31 are shown but empty)
- Columns (16 columns total): Represent metadata register bit-field segments
- Each cell contains: lane_id : start_col - end_col

The notation in each cell is: LANE:START_COL-END_COL
This means thread lane LANE holds metadata bits covering columns START_COL through END_COL.

==============================================================
BIT-WIDTH INDICATORS (shown below the grid)
==============================================================

Four horizontal arrows indicate groupings of columns by bit width:
- 4-bits:  spans columns 0-3 (first column group only)
- 8-bits:  spans columns 0-7 (first two column groups)
- 16-bits: spans columns 0-15 (first four column groups)
- 32-bits: spans columns 0-31 (first eight column groups)

These indicate that each cell entry represents 4 bits of metadata, and groupings of 1, 2, 4, or 8 cells correspond to 4, 8, 16, or 32 bits respectively.

==============================================================
COMPLETE CELL CONTENTS (Lane : Start_Col - End_Col)
==============================================================

The grid has 16 active rows (lanes 0-15) and 16 columns.
Each cell format: LANE:START-END

Col 2:  4-7
Col 3:  8-11

Col 5:  16-19

Col 7:  24-27
Col 8:  28-31
Col 9:  32-35
Col 10: 36-39
Col 11: 40-43
Col 12: 44-47
Col 13: 48-51
Col 14: 52-55
Col 15: 56-59

SPARSITY METADATA LAYOUT FIGURE DESCRIPTION

Caption: Sparsity Metadata Layout for M = 128 / M = 256 for .kind::f8f6f4, .kind::mxf8f6f4, .kind::i8, .kind::mxf4, .kind::mxf4nvf4

OVERALL STRUCTURE:
- The table has 32 rows (lanes/threads 0 through 31, labeled on the left)
- The table has 16 columns of cells
- Each cell contains a notation in the format: lane:start_col-end_col
- The legend at the bottom right confirms: "lane : start_col - end_col"

COLUMN BIT-WIDTH ANNOTATIONS (shown below the table with arrows):
- First column (leftmost): 4-bits
- First two columns: 8-bits
- First four columns: 16-bits
- First eight columns: 32-bits

These annotations indicate that groups of columns correspond to 4-bit, 8-bit, 16-bit, and 32-bit register widths respectively.

CELL FORMAT EXPLANATION:
Each cell is labeled as "L:S-E" where:
- L = lane number (thread index, 0-31)
- S = start column (bit/column index start)
- E = end column (bit/column index end)
